ASSIGNED_TO.description: App is assigned to principals approximating accessible users
ASSIGNED_TO.details: Gated behind reachability thresholds. Higher weights apply when assignment count exceeds thresholds. If the app does NOT require assignment and has zero explicit assignments, it is broadly reachable (any user can consent/sign-in). Treat as high risk via BROAD_REACHABILITY instead.

BROAD_REACHABILITY.description: No assignments but appRoleAssignmentRequired=false implies broad reach
BROAD_REACHABILITY.details: Applied when app does not require assignment and has no explicit assignments, making it broadly reachable (any user can consent/sign-in). High reachability can also occur when assignment is required AND assignments are large (direct + group-expanded). Thresholds: >50 (groups-only), >100 (users-only or mixed). Prefer true effective user count via group expansion where possible.

PRIVILEGE.description: Directory roles reachable from app depends on assignments

DECEPTION.description: Unverified publisher with name mismatch between publisher and display name
DECEPTION.details: Applied when publisher is unverified AND there is a significant mismatch between publisherName and appDisplayName, suggesting potential deceptive naming. Verification context includes checking if verifiedPublisher is null/empty and comparing name strings for similarity. GATED: Only applied when total_urls > 0 (apps with reply URLs capable of OAuth flows) and not for well-known Microsoft platform apps.

MIXED_REPLYURL_DOMAINS.identity_laundering_description: Identity laundering signal: reply URLs use domains not aligned with homepage/branding
MIXED_REPLYURL_DOMAINS.attribution_ambiguity_description: Attribution ambiguity: multiple distinct domains in reply URLs
MIXED_REPLYURL_DOMAINS.details: Multiple eTLD+1 values is a weak signal (locales/admin portals are common). Only elevate when an outlier domain is unrelated to primary vendor domain(s). GATED: Only applied when total_urls > 0. Uses RDAP/WHOIS enrichment to verify if domains belong to the same organization (with name normalization) to prevent false positives.

PUBLIC_CLIENT_FLOW_RISK.implicit_flow_description: Implicit flow enabled (access token or ID token issuance)
PUBLIC_CLIENT_FLOW_RISK.details: Public clients and implicit flow cannot securely store secrets, relying solely on redirect URIs for security. This increases risk of token interception and misuse.

GOVERNANCE.description: Assignments not required for app governance

UNVERIFIED_PUBLISHER.details: Service principal has no verifiedPublisherId (unverified publisher). Alone is moderate risk; combine with other signals (privileged scopes, broad reachability, URL anomalies). GATED: Not applied to Internal apps (appOwnership='Internal') as tenant-owned apps don't require publisher verification.

OFFLINE_ACCESS_PERSISTENCE.details: App requests offline_access (delegated) or otherwise gains refresh-token style persistence. This is persistence risk, not impersonation by itself.

CREDENTIALS_PRESENT.details: Service principal has keyCredentials and/or passwordCredentials present (including 'hidden' secrets where value is not retrievable). Raises persistence and lateral reuse risk.

PASSWORD_CREDENTIALS_PRESENT.details: Service principal has passwordCredentials present. Password secrets are easier to exfiltrate/misuse than certs; treat as higher risk than keyCredentials alone.

IDENTITY_LAUNDERING.details: App appears 'Microsoft-owned' in appOwnerOrganizationId/publisherName (e.g., Microsoft Accounts/Services) while not being a Microsoft first-party app. Indicates ownership attribution is obscured for unverified multi-tenant apps. GATED: Only applied when total_urls > 0 (apps with reply URLs capable of OAuth flows where attribution confusion matters to users).

REPLYURL_OUTLIER_DOMAIN.details: One or more replyUrls are on a domain that is not in the main vendor domain set (eTLD+1 cluster) for the app. This is stronger than simply 'multiple domains'. GATED: Only applied when total_urls > 0. Uses RDAP/WHOIS enrichment to verify if domains belong to the same organization (with name normalization) to prevent false positives.

REPLY_URL_ANOMALIES.details: Reply URLs contain security anomalies such as non-HTTPS URLs, IP literals, localhost, punycode, or wildcards. GATED: Only applied when total_urls > 0.

notes.sourceScope: Graph-only scoring. Sign-in logs excluded by design at this stage.
notes.assignments: Assignment reachability should be computed using effective user count via group expansion where possible.
